summary: Analyzed 12 days of memory logs revealing peak usage at 86% with swap reaching 72%. Identified conflict between earlyoom and systemd-oomd, missing zram configuration, and suboptimal vm.swappiness. Recommended disabling systemd-oomd, enabling zram with zstd compression, and lowering swappiness to 10.

artifacts.summary: Comprehensive memory analysis with NixOS configuration recommendations for zram, sysctl tuning, and OOM killer consolidation

key_findings: Peak memory 86% on Feb 12 triggered earlyoom intervention
Two OOM killers running concurrently (earlyoom + systemd-oomd)
zram not enabled - missing compression benefits
vm.swappiness at default 60 - too high for desktop
Claude processes can reach 1.4GB each with 5+ concurrent instances
Average memory usage 54% indicates headroom for optimization
